title: A Wisconsin Farm Bred 1,635 Beagles for Drug Testing. It's Shutting Down.
script: Who breeds 1,635 beagles? Ridglan Farms in Blue Mounds, Wisconsin did — not as pets, but as research inventory for pharmaceutical companies. Beagles are used in drug testing because of their size, docility, and predictable genetics. This Wisconsin farm bred and supplied thousands of them to labs across the country. Then inspectors found 311 violations: unsanitary conditions, inadequate veterinary care, animals suffering without intervention. Animal rights groups sued. Ridglan eventually agreed to give up its breeding license by July 1, 2026 and hand over all remaining dogs. 1,635 beagles — each one bred to become a test subject — are now being transferred through rescue groups. Most have never lived outside a kennel. Most have never seen a yard. So: how many other Ridglan Farms are still operating right now?
